ordered_steps.name: Load approved P3-SPEC-ELEMENTS review and canonical projection rule
Load approved interaction messages and closure message coverage
Identify semantic reuse candidates from canonical element ids and message ids
Accept reuse only when semantic role, data, operations, state, hierarchy, interaction, constraints, and trace refs align
Record non-reuse reasons when similar surfaces differ semantically
Assign presentation modes based on semantic intent, trigger, scope, lifecycle, recoverability, and decision need
Ensure all approved interaction messages are covered and no unknown message ref is used
Require approved consistency exceptions for equivalent information using different modes
Create handoff for layout with pattern ids and presentation decisions but no layout placement decisions

pattern_rules: Pattern reuse is semantic, not visual.
Every shared pattern must include semantic role, data structure, operation set, state model, information hierarchy, interaction model, surface constraints, reuse scope, and trace refs.
Every reuse_scope entry must resolve to a canonical P3 element id, approved interaction message id, or approved exception ref.
Blocked, open-gap, rejected, superseded, structural-review-only, and inductive-unapproved elements cannot be pattern consumers.
LAYOUT_PATTERN rows are semantic pre-layout groupings and cannot define carrier, breakpoint, containment, order, scroll, width, height, or z-axis behavior.
Every approved interaction message must have presentation coverage.
Presentation decisions with equivalent semantic intent, trigger condition, scope, and lifecycle must use one mode unless an approved exception allows the difference.
Sustained, persistent, audit, session, processing, or user-decision information cannot rely on transient-only unrecoverable presentation.
Patterns and presentation decisions cannot create new product capability or new elements.
Downstream layout may reference pattern ids but cannot invent ids outside the registry.
forbidden_behaviors: reuse components because they look similar, share a color, share an icon, or share visual styling only
consume projection-excluded element ids as reusable pattern scope
reference unknown interaction messages from presentation decisions
use different presentation modes for equivalent information without an approved exception
hide sustained or decision-critical information in an unrecoverable toast-only surface
define carrier-specific layout, breakpoint behavior, containment hierarchy, ordering, scroll rules, width rules, height rules, or z-axis layering
create repository implementation code during this spec workpack
